records.ety: [L. depositus, p. p. of deponere. See Depone, and cf. Deposit, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Deposited; p. pr. & vb. n. Depositing.]

defs: To lay down; to place; to put; to let fall or throw down (as sediment); as, a crocodile deposits her eggs in the sand; the waters deposited a rich alluvium.
quotes: The fear is deposited in conscience.

defs: To lay up or away for safe keeping; to put up; to store; as, to deposit goods in a warehouse.
To lodge in some one's hands for safe keeping; to commit to the custody of another; to intrust; esp., to place in a bank, as a sum of money subject to order.
To lay aside; to rid one's self of.
quotes: If what is written prove useful to you, to the depositing that which I can not but deem an error.
notes: ☞ Both this verb and the noun following were formerly written deposite.

records.ety: [L. depositum, fr. depositus, p. p. of deponere: cf. F. dpt, OF. depost. See Deposit, v. t., and cf. Depot.]

defs: That which is deposited, or laid or thrown down; as, a deposit in a flue; especially, matter precipitated from a solution (as the siliceous deposits of hot springs), or that which is mechanically deposited (as the mud, gravel, etc., deposits of a river).
quotes: The deposit already formed affording to the succeeding portion of the charged fluid a basis.

defs: A natural occurrence of a useful mineral under the conditions to invite exploitation.

That which is placed anywhere, or in any one's hands, for safe keeping; something intrusted to the care of another; esp., money lodged with a bank or banker, subject to order; anything given as pledge or security.
A bailment of money or goods to be kept gratuitously for the bailor.
Money lodged with a party as earnest or security for the performance of a duty assumed by the person depositing.
A place of deposit; a depository.
